claim: For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^3 - n equals the product of the three consecutive integers (n-1), n, and (n+1).
For all integers n, the product n(n+1) is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^2 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^4 - n^2 is divisible by 12.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 5.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 7.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 11.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 66.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 13.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 5.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 7.
For all integers n, n^13 - n is divisible by 2730.
For all integers n, gcd(n, n+1) = 1.
For all integers n, gcd(n, n+2) is either 1 or 2.
For all odd integers n, gcd(n, n+2) = 1.
For all even integers n, gcd(n, n+2) = 2.
For all odd integers n, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 8.
For all integers n not divisible by 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 3.
For all integers n coprime to 6, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24.
For every prime n greater than 3, n^2 - 1 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, n and n^2 have the same parity.
For all integers n, n and n^4 have the same parity.
For all integers n, n and n^3 have the same parity.
For every even integer n, n^2 is divisible by 4.
For all integers n, n(n+1)(2n+1) is divisible by 6.
For all integers n >= 1, the sum of squares 1^2+2^2+...+n^2 equals n(n+1)(2n+1)/6.
For every integer n > 2, phi(n) is even.
For every prime n, phi(n) = n - 1.
For every prime n, sigma(n) = n + 1.
For every prime n, tau(n) = 2.
For every prime n greater than 2, phi(n) + sigma(n) = 2n.
For every integer n > 0, tau(n) is odd if and only if n is a perfect square.
For every odd perfect-square integer n, n is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all integers n, n^9 - n is divisible by 9.
For every prime n, sigma(n^2) = 1 + n + n^2.
For every prime n, tau(n^2) = 3.
For every prime n, phi(n) * sigma(n) = n^2 - 1.
